title: Ingress, Gateway API 1.5.1, Routing, TLS, Delegation, and Status · Object and Event Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective Gateway API routing and delegation policy; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in ingress, gateway api 1.5.1, routing, tls, delegation, and status, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in Gateway API routing and delegation policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries.
Select GatewayClass and implementation from controller identity, parameters, supported features, conformance reports, release channel, and operational ownership.
Design Gateway addresses and listeners with protocol, port, hostname, namespace attachment, certificate reference, TLS mode, and conflict behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence. Competency probe: Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries.

Inspect the K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare service, ingress, implementation-specific annotations, gateway api, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries. For Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary, keep K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-referencegrant-status-conformance-9eqisw". Tailor its gateway-route.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance into observable performance evidence. content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-referencegrant-status-conformance-9eqisw
  - competency: k8s-referencegrant-status-conformance
    artifact: gateway-route.yaml
    decision: referencegrant status conformance
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it
    retained-proof: Pod state previous exit endpoint readiness and request result
    case-key: 9eqisw
    change: |
      gatewayClassName: conformant-public
      listener: https-status-example-test
      tls-certificate: platform/status-cert
      route: intake/status-route
      backend: intake/status-api:8080
      referenceGrant: platform-to-intake
      status: Accepted-Programmed-ResolvedRefs
    verify: kubectl get gateway,httproute,referencegrant -A -o json
    failure: Reject controller-free Gateway assumptions, cross-namespace references without grants, rule-order myths, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership.
    repair: Select a conformant implementation, bind listeners and Routes, authorize references, inspect observedGeneration conditions, and transfer-test traffic.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Authorize cross-namespace references with ReferenceGrant and verify Accepted, Programmed, ResolvedRefs, observedGeneration, attached routes, and implementation conformance.
